meta.target_audience: Urban professionals 28–42, prioritise provenance and craft over convenience, already buy direct-trade beans, follow roasters on Instagram, read Standart magazine, treat a Saturday morning cup as a ritual rather than a caffeine fix.

creative_direction.concept: Every bag of coffee is a compressed journey from a specific hillside to your cup — the site makes that altitude, terroir, and human chain viscerally felt.
creative_direction.narrative: The visitor lands on darkness broken only by steam curling off a single pour — no text, just atmosphere. Scrolling down reveals the origin map: exact farm coordinates, elevation, harvest date. The middle act introduces the roasters by name, with their hand-annotated tasting notes. The site closes on the subscription CTA framed not as a product but as 'a standing order from the mountain.'
creative_direction.emotional_tone: The quiet pride of someone who has found exactly the thing they were looking for.
creative_direction.signature_moment: On the origin section, hovering a farm pin on the map triggers a 3-second ambient field recording from that specific farm (birds, wind, distant machinery) while the elevation number counts up in large type — making the geography audible.
creative_direction.brand_personality: A seasoned Q-grader who speaks in exact descriptors ('dried apricot, brown butter, 72% cacao') rather than superlatives. Would never say 'artisanal' or 'passionate'. Comfortable with silence. Wears an apron with burn marks.

sitemap.label: Opening
sitemap.purpose: Create immediate atmosphere; communicate that this is not a commodity brand — zero copy, pure sensation.

sitemap.label: Where It Grows
sitemap.purpose: Build credibility and emotional connection through farm-level provenance — interactive map with audio.

children.label: Farm Map
children.purpose: Let visitors explore the sourcing geography and trigger farm audio snippets.

children.label: Farm Profile
children.purpose: Slide-in panel with farmer portrait, elevation, processing method, and harvest window.

sitemap.label: The People
sitemap.purpose: Humanise the craft; introduce the two roasters by name with handwritten tasting notes.

sitemap.label: Current Roasts
sitemap.purpose: Convert interest into selection — present 3–4 active single-origins with buy/subscribe actions.

sitemap.label: Standing Order
sitemap.purpose: Drive recurring revenue; reframe subscription as an ongoing relationship, not a discount programme.

sitemap.label: Footer
sitemap.purpose: Wholesale enquiries, press, and the only social link on the site (Instagram).

ux_structure.conversion_flow: Hero creates atmosphere → origins section earns trust through provenance specificity → roasters section adds human accountability → offerings lets them pick a bag → subscription CTA captures them for repeat revenue. The path from hero to 'add to cart' is achievable in 4 scroll steps with no lateral navigation required.
ux_structure.navigation_pattern: Wordmark only in top-left, no nav links on desktop — the page rewards linear scrolling. A minimal dot-indicator on the right edge marks section position. At ≤768px a 24px hamburger opens a full-screen overlay nav with all section labels.

components.name: FarmPin
components.description: SVG map marker that pulses on hover, triggers audio playback and a slide-in FarmPanel. Active state shows elevation label in Canela display size.

components.name: FarmPanel
components.description: Slide-in right-side drawer showing farmer portrait (greyscale), farm name, country, altitude in masl, processing method badge, and harvest month range.

components.name: RoasterCard
components.description: Portrait photo in a ragged-edge mask, name in Canela h3, handwriting-style SVG of a tasting note overlaid at 40° rotation.

components.name: CoffeeCard
components.description: Product card with origin flag, process badge (washed/natural/honey), flavour wheel fragment, price, and two CTAs: 'Buy once' and 'Add to standing order'.

components.name: ElevationCounter
components.description: Large Canela display-size number that counts up from 0 to the farm's actual masl value over 2.4s when the farm pin is activated.

components.name: AmbientAudioPlayer
components.description: Invisible audio element, auto-plays on farm pin hover, fades out after 3s or on mouseout. No visible controls — sound is the surprise.

components.name: SectionDotNav
components.description: Vertical stack of 6 dots on the right edge, active dot fills with Blonde Roast accent colour. Clicks scroll to the corresponding section.

components.name: SubscriptionToggle
components.description: Segmented control: 'Single bag' / 'Standing order'. Selecting standing order exposes frequency selector (2-weekly / 4-weekly) and shows a small price-per-bag saving.

global_principles: Nothing moves unless scroll or pointer caused it — no auto-playing carousels or looping animations.
Easing is always decelerating (ease-out or custom cubic-bezier) — the site never feels rushed.
Opacity and transform are the only animated properties; no layout-triggering animations.
Reduced-motion media query disables all transitions and parallax, substituting instant cuts.

per_section.scroll_behavior: Steam particle canvas parallaxes at 0.25× scroll rate. Background stays fixed. At 80vh scroll depth, the canvas opacity fades to 0 over 40px.
per_section.transitions: On load: full-screen black fades to scene over 1800ms after a 400ms initial hold. Wordmark fades in from opacity 0 at translateY(12px) after 1200ms.

per_section.scroll_behavior: Section pins for 150vh of scroll travel. During the pin, the SVG world map scales from 0.85 to 1.0 and farm pins stagger-fade in at 80ms intervals.
per_section.transitions: Section enter: map container clips in from bottom using clip-path(inset(100% 0 0 0)) → inset(0%). Farm pins scale from 0 to 1 with a spring overshoot.
per_section.timing: Map clip: 700ms cubic-bezier(0.16, 1, 0.3, 1). Pin spring: 500ms cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1).

per_section.scroll_behavior: Drawer is pointer-triggered, not scroll-driven. Slides in from right at translateX(100%) → translateX(0).
per_section.transitions: Panel open: translateX + overlay fade-in simultaneously. Close: reverse, 50ms faster.

per_section.scroll_behavior: Two roaster cards enter from opposite sides (left and right) as the section scrolls into the viewport center.
per_section.transitions: Left card: translateX(-60px) opacity 0 → natural. Right card: translateX(60px) opacity 0 → natural. Tasting-note SVG overlays rotate from -50° → -40° while fading in.

per_section.scroll_behavior: Cards stagger-enter from below as they cross the 80% viewport threshold. No scroll pinning.
per_section.transitions: Each CoffeeCard: translateY(32px) opacity 0 → natural, 80ms stagger per card.

per_section.scroll_behavior: Section background fades from Wet Earth to a slightly lighter Graphite as it enters, giving a subtle 'stepping into a warmer room' feel.
per_section.transitions: Headline enters at translateY(20px) opacity 0. Toggle and frequency selector fade in 200ms later.

scenes.description: Hero background: a shallow field of volumetric steam particles rising from below the frame. Rendered on a dark canvas; particles are off-white with additive blending. Responds to pointer position with a subtle 6° tilt via device orientation or mouse parallax.
scenes.camera_movement: Fixed. Pointer moves shift a secondary layer of particles on a separate plane at 0.3× rate — creating depth without a moving camera.

images.prompt: Extreme close-up of a slow-motion pour of black coffee into a white ceramic cup, steam rising, dark background, shot on 85mm f/1.4, shallow depth of field, slightly underexposed, editorial photography style
images.style_reference: Charlie Schuck food photography — deep shadow, single key light from above-left

images.prompt: Wide establishing shot of a coffee farm in the Ethiopian highlands at golden hour, red coffee cherries on branches in foreground, terraced hillside and mist in background, shot on medium format film
images.style_reference: Sebastião Salgado — high contrast black and white with luminous midtones
images.usage: Farm detail panel portrait, origins section

images.prompt: Portrait of a Colombian coffee farmer holding a basket of freshly picked red cherries, direct eye contact, natural afternoon light, documentary style, slightly desaturated
images.usage: Farm detail panel portrait, origins section

images.prompt: Black and white portrait of a woman in her late 30s standing in front of a drum roaster, wearing a canvas apron, arms crossed, confident and calm expression, soft directional studio light
images.style_reference: Platon portraiture — simple background, strong eye contact

images.prompt: Black and white portrait of a man in his early 40s adjusting a roaster dial, profile shot, industrial background slightly blurred, hands sharp, technical focus
Studio product shot of a matte kraft paper coffee bag with minimal black label, bag slightly open showing beans, white seamless background, soft shadow, top-down 15° angle
images.style_reference: Apple product photography — crisp, no lens flare, zero noise

images.prompt: Same studio setup as product-bag-yirgacheffe but bag label shows Colombia Huila text, bag upright and sealed

videos.prompt: 10-second seamless loop of coffee steam rising in slow motion against a near-black background, shallow depth of field, no camera movement, photorealistic
videos.usage: Hero section background fallback if WebGL particle scene underperforms on mobile

audio.description: 3-second ambient field recording from an Ethiopian highland farm: morning birds, light wind through coffee cherry branches, distant rooster
audio.usage: Triggered on hover of Ethiopia farm pin in origins map

audio.description: 3-second ambient field recording from a Colombian finca: cicadas, water channel, a brief snippet of someone calling out in Spanish at low volume
audio.usage: Triggered on hover of Colombia farm pin in origins map

creative_direction.notes: The signature moment (audio + elevation counter) is distinctive and hard to copy — high recall value. Score held back by the narrative not yet specifying how the transition from roasters section to offerings avoids feeling like a gear-shift into commerce.

visual_design.notes: Canela + Söhne pairing is well-worn in the specialty food space but executed with the right weight choices. Dried Cherry highlight is underused — currently only planned for the farm-audio active state, should appear in at least one more place to feel intentional.

motion.notes: Global principles are disciplined. The 150vh pin on origins is confident. Risk: on mid-range Android, the particle canvas and the pinned scroll section running together may cause jank — needs a performance branch where the pin is replaced with a standard scroll-triggered stagger.

assets.notes: Prompts are specific and have clear style references. The two farm-audio clips are the strongest asset idea in the brief. Gap: no asset defined for the standing-order section — it currently relies entirely on typography and component interaction, which may feel thin.

final.notes: Not yet scored — awaiting build completion.

version_history.summary: Initial brief created from client questionnaire responses for Altitude Coffee Roasters project.
Concept, narrative arc, and signature moment defined; brand personality deliberately positioned against 'artisanal' category clichés.
Sitemap locked at 6 sections; no top navigation by design — linear scroll with dot indicator only.
Palette anchored to roast-spectrum colours; Dried Cherry highlight flagged as underused in taste review.
Per-section motion spec written; performance risk flagged on Ethiopia origins pin with 150vh pin + particle canvas co-running.
7 images, 1 video, 2 audio assets specified; gap noted in subscription section asset coverage.
